column4: older ruling (RTR 20010710) appears superseded by .1 (LSJ 20090115-2, opposite pool-burn timing) — flag for review against current card text

This card's own zero-blood torpid-Prince interaction; fails transfer — no other card shares this effect chain. Nearest 5.8 (titles) since the outcome turns on Prince status, though 5.8 doesn't claim this specific effect
Confirms this master card carries no vampire requirement, matching the default master-phase-action rule; restates known default — fails non-obviousness
Confirms this card's own removal effect is immediate, not delayed; card-specific timing, fails transfer. Nearest 1.11 (ash heap) though it doesn't own removal-effect timing

Classifies this card as a non-derivative hunting ground for {Poacher's Hunting Ground}'s counting purposes; card-pair-specific, fails transfer — nothing else in the taxonomy owns hunting-ground classification
No section owns library-cut mechanics (how a Methuselah may divide/cut a deck being manipulated); rare mechanic, nothing in the taxonomy claims deck-cutting procedure
Clarifies uniqueness creates no exception to the infernal-torpor prerequisite already stated in .8; narrow edge case restating that rule, fails transfer beyond this ability
Classifies this card's provided action as an "equip action"; card-specific classification, fails transfer to a different card
Restates this card's own controller-match requirement between the card and its target vampire; fails non-obviousness — "you control" wording already implies this

No section covers default visibility of library-search results (hidden from other Methuselahs by default). Nearest 3.7.3 (equip actions, this card's own action type) though it doesn't claim search visibility. Shares the gap with Vaticination.1 (replacement draws)
No section states whether a replacement draw is shown only to the drawing player or to all; nearest 1.9 (replacement) though its scope doesn't claim visibility. Shares the gap with Vast Wealth.6
!TENSION:no-effect-plays — here a no-effect action does NOT lock the acting vampire, unlike the general rule that any attempted action locks regardless of effect
!TENSION:no-effect-plays — dodge remains playable even though it accomplishes nothing against this effect
Confirms this vampire can play cards requiring two specific disciplines at once; trivially follows from having both disciplines, fails non-obviousness

Sect-as-a-trait gap (watch-list, per taxonomy): nothing owns sect classification / eligibility for sect-keyed effects like {Anarch Manifesto, An}; 5.7 (traits) and 5.8 (titles/sect change) both touch it, neither claims it
Classifies this card's provided action as a hunt action; card-specific classification, fails transfer to a different card
Classifies this card's action as undirected; card-specific classification, fails transfer to a different card

!TENSION:abnormal-entry-requirements — this ally/retainer burn-requirement applies even through abnormal entry (eg. {Pressing Flesh}), pulling against 1.6's general rule that abnormal entry ignores requirements
This card's own once-per-turn-per-Tremere blood-transfer limit; fails transfer beyond this location's own text
Purely administrative: flags a 2007 card-text change invalidating older rulings; not a rule at all

No section states that non-action means of putting a vampire into play (eg. recruit-like abilities) don't count as an "action" for action-keyed effects like {Blood Cult Awareness Network}'s stealth reduction; nearest 3.7.4 (recruit action), though it only covers the action itself
